summary: Phase 2 (Light Mode Full Coverage) + Phase 3 (Trust & Conversion) testing. Light mode fully verified across all platform surfaces. Homepage trust badges + testimonials confirmed. Login page Australian Hosted badge confirmed. All regression tests pass. CRITICAL: New Pricing.js component with annual/monthly toggle, comparison table, and trust badges was created but the /pricing route was NOT updated — old SitePricingPage still serves at /pricing. All Pricing Phase 3 features are unreachable.

ui_bugs.issue: Pricing.js (with billing-toggle, annual/monthly toggle, comparison table, trust badges) is imported in App.js but NOT routed to any URL. /pricing route still serves old SitePricingPage (PricingPage.js). All Phase 3 Pricing features are completely unreachable.
ui_bugs.priority: CRITICAL — 1-line fix: change <Route path='/pricing' element={<SitePricingPage />} /> to <Route path='/pricing' element={<Pricing />} /> in App.js

issues: Pricing.js uses hardcoded dark colors (e.g., background: '#0A0F18', '#141C26', '#243140') — NOT CSS vars. Will NOT support light mode if routed. May be intentional for public marketing page.

design_issues.screen: Pricing.js trust badges vs review spec
issues: Pricing.js TRUST_BADGES[2].label = 'Zero Data Leakage' but review request expects '🛡️ Privacy Act'. Minor content discrepancy from the spec.

action_items: CRITICAL: In /app/frontend/src/App.js, change route from '<Route path="/pricing" element={<SitePricingPage />} />' to '<Route path="/pricing" element={<Pricing />} />'. The new Pricing.js component (with annual/monthly toggle, comparison table, trust badges) is imported but has NO route — it's dead code.
MINOR: Pricing.js TRUST_BADGES[2].label is 'Zero Data Leakage' (🛡️) but review spec expected 'Privacy Act'. Align if needed.
MINOR: Pricing.js uses hardcoded dark colors (not CSS vars) — will NOT support light mode. Intentional for public marketing page or needs to be updated like other 63 files.
NOTE: Once routing is fixed, verify Pricing.js features: billing-toggle, annual prices ($39/$79/$159), comparison table (5 columns, 12 rows), savings badges
critical_code_review_comments: App.js line 169: '<Route path="/pricing" element={<SitePricingPage />} />' — New Pricing component (imported on line 32) was never routed. All Phase 3 pricing features are unreachable. This is the only blocker.

Pricing.js uses hardcoded inline style dark colors: background '#0A0F18', '#141C26', border '#243140' — NOT CSS vars. These will NOT respond to [data-theme='light']. Consistent with other website pages (HomePage.js also uses #07121E) which appear to be dark-only by design.
Light mode CSS var approach is working correctly. All 743-replacement effects are verified on the app platform surfaces (sidebar, main content, cards). CSS overrides in index.css are effective.

success_rate.frontend: 72% (13/18 tests pass; 5 fail due to single routing bug in App.js)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 light mode: ALL PASS (advisor/revenue/risk/market/operations/integrations/settings all light, sidebar light, cards white, text dark). Phase 3: Homepage trust badges + testimonials PASS, Login Australian Hosted PASS, all regressions PASS. Only blocker: Pricing.js (new annual/monthly toggle) has NO route — /pricing still shows old SitePricingPage. After main agent fixes route (1-line change in App.js), retest: billing-toggle, annual prices ($49→$39, $99→$79, $199→$159), comparison table expand (5 cols, 12 rows), trust badges on pricing page.

pricing_routing_bug.symptom: Navigating to /pricing shows old 'Multiply the Capability of Your Entire Team' page with $750/$1,950 prices, no billing toggle, no comparison table
pricing_routing_bug.root_cause: In App.js, line 32 imports new Pricing component ('import Pricing from "./pages/Pricing"') but line 169 routes '/pricing' to SitePricingPage ('from pages/website/PricingPage.js'). The import on line 32 is unused dead code.
pricing_routing_bug.fix: App.js line 169: Change element={<SitePricingPage />} to element={<Pricing />}
pricing_routing_bug.impact: All Phase 3 pricing features (annual/monthly toggle, comparison table, 5-plan grid with $39/$79/$159 prices, trust badges) are completely unreachable
